FX SVG FILTER API. Vector filter layer of the element renderer — applied before rasterization.
FX SVG FILTER API. Numeric input for this filter row; safe to explore, values only affect display, never your project data.
FX SVG FILTER API. Numeric input for this filter row; combine with the neighbouring cells for compound effects.
FX SVG FILTER API. Numeric input for this filter row; 0 leaves the stage neutral.
FX SVG FILTER API. Numeric input for this filter row; extreme values are clamped by the renderer.
FX SVG FILTER API. Second vector filter row — same principle as above, independent values.
FX SVG FILTER API. Numeric input for this filter row.

FX HTML CANVAS API. Fifth canvas parameter group — highest indices of the drawing layer.
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 20
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 21
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 22

FX HTML CANVAS API. Fourth canvas parameter group.
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 15

FX HTML CANVAS API. Third canvas parameter group.
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 10

FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer. => Canvas Params 9
FX HTML CANVAS API. Core drawing setup — the raster layer every element starts from.
FX HTML CANVAS API. Sets the base draw style of the element. => 0=Square, 1=Round, 2=Stroke, 3=Strokes
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer; effect depends on the draw style chosen above. => Canvas Params 1
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer; effect depends on the draw style chosen above. => Canvas Params 2
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer; effect depends on the draw style chosen above. => Canvas Params 3
FX HTML CANVAS API. Numeric parameter of the canvas renderer; effect depends on the draw style chosen above. => Canvas Params 4
FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. WebGL post-processing applied on top of the rendered element.

FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. Selects the filter; 0 bypasses this layer entirely. => 0=None, 1=Ascii, 2=Noise, 3=Dot, 4=CRT, 5=Simplex, 6=Glow, 7=Kawase, 8=Advanced, 9=ZoomBlur, 10=Twist, 11=BulgePinch, 12=Glitch, 13=Reflection
FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. Fine-tunes the selected filter — its meaning shifts with the choice above (size, strength, radius, or offset). => Pixijs Params 1
FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. Fine-tunes the selected filter — its meaning shifts with the choice above. => Pixijs Params 2
FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. Fine-tunes the selected filter — its meaning shifts with the choice above. => Pixijs Params 3
FX PIXIJS PARAMETERS. Fine-tunes the selected filter — its meaning shifts with the choice above. => Pixijs Params 4
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Non-photorealistic rendering pass applied to the exported image.
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Selects the effect; 0 bypasses this layer entirely. => 0=None, 1=Stylization, 2=Detail Enhance, 3=Pencil Sketch, 4=Cartoonify
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Tuning value for the selected effect — typically smoothing strength or edge emphasis. => OpenCV Params 1
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Tuning value for the selected effect. => OpenCV Params 2
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Tuning value for the selected effect. => OpenCV Params 3
FX OPENCV PARAMETERS. Tuning value for the selected effect. => OpenCV Params 4

3-1: CSS LAYOUT FLEX-GROW / SHRINK. Controls how the element claims or yields space within its flex container — 0 keeps it rigid.
CSS LAYOUT WIDTH. Base width of the element; the flex and min/max constraints below may override it.
CSS LAYOUT MAX- MIN-WIDTH. Clamps the width — the element never exceeds the max nor collapses below the min.
CSS LAYOUT HEIGHT. Base height of the element.
CSS LAYOUT MAX- MIN-HEIGHT. Clamps the height, analogous to the width bounds above.
CSS LAYOUT MIN-HEIGHT ASPECT-RATIO. Locks the element's proportions; works against the min-height constraint when the two compete.
CSS LAYOUT ALIGN-SELF. Cross-axis alignment of this element, overriding the container default. => start, ends, center, stretch
CSS LAYOUT MARGIN-LEFT -RIGHT. Horizontal alignment via auto margins — a selector, not a distance; use the plain CSS MARGIN below for numeric spacing. => start, ends, center
CSS LAYOUT ORDER. Visual position in the flex flow, independent of the row order in this sheet — lower values render first.
CSS LAYOUT LEFT POSITION. Horizontal offset of the element from its reference edge.
CSS LAYOUT TOP POSITION. Vertical offset of the element from its reference edge.
CSS LAYOUT LEFT WIDTH. Horizontal extent paired with LEFT POSITION — together they pin the element's placement along the x-axis.
CSS LAYOUT TOP HEIGHT. Vertical extent paired with TOP POSITION — the y-axis counterpart.
CSS MARGIN-LEFT -RIGHT. Numeric outer spacing on the horizontal sides — pushes neighbours away.
CSS MARGIN-TOP -BOTTOM. Numeric outer spacing on the vertical sides.
CSS PADDING-LEFT -RIGHT. Inner spacing on the horizontal sides — insets the content from the element's own edge.
CSS PADDING-TOP BOTTOM. Inner spacing on the horizontal sides — insets the content from the element's own edge.
HTML RELATION ENTITY. Assigns the element to its parent container in the layout tree — this reference decides which flex context all properties above operate in.
CSS LAYOUT WIDTH. Base width of the element; the min/max constraints below may override it.
CSS LAYOUT MAX- MIN-WIDTH. Clamps the width — the element never exceeds the max nor collapses below the min.
CSS LAYOUT HEIGHT. Base height of the element.
CSS LAYOUT MAX- MIN-HEIGHT. Clamps the height, analogous to the width bounds above.
CSS LAYOUT MIN-HEIGHT ASPECT-RATIO. Locks the element's proportions; works against the min-height constraint when the two compete.
CSS LAYOUT MARGIN-TOP -BOTTOM. Vertical alignment via auto margins — a selector, not a distance; use the plain CSS MARGIN below for numeric spacing. => start, ends, center
CSS LAYOUT MARGIN-LEFT -RIGHT. Horizontal alignment via auto margins — the x-axis counterpart of the selector above. => start, ends, center
CSS LAYOUT Z-INDEX. Stacking order relative to overlapping elements — higher values render on top.
CSS LAYOUT LEFT POSITION. Horizontal offset of the element from its reference edge.
CSS LAYOUT TOP POSITION. Vertical offset of the element from its reference edge.
CSS LAYOUT LEFT WIDTH. Horizontal extent paired with LEFT POSITION — together they pin the element's placement along the x-axis.
CSS LAYOUT TOP HEIGHT. Vertical extent paired with TOP POSITION — the y-axis counterpart.
CSS MARGIN-LEFT -RIGHT. Numeric outer spacing on the horizontal sides — pushes neighbours away.
CSS MARGIN-TOP -BOTTOM. Numeric outer spacing on the vertical sides.
CSS PADDING-LEFT -RIGHT. Inner spacing on the horizontal sides — insets the content from the element's own edge.
CSS PADDING-TOP -BOTTOM. Inner spacing on the vertical sides.
HTML RELATION ENTITY. Assigns the element to its parent container in the layout tree — reparenting moves the element and all its spacing into a new context.
3-2: CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. First shadow layer — horizontal offset.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. First shadow layer — vertical offset.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. First shadow layer — blur radius; higher values soften the edge.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. First shadow layer — spread radius; grows or shrinks the shadow body.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. First shadow layer — inset toggle; casts the shadow inward instead of outward.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Second shadow layer — horizontal offset; layers stack on top of the first.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Second shadow layer — vertical offset.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Second shadow layer — blur radius.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Second shadow layer — spread radius.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Second shadow layer — inset toggle.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE -POSITION. Horizontal sizing of the background image within the element.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE -POSITION. Vertical sizing of the background image.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE -POSITION. Horizontal placement of the background within the element.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE -POSITION. Vertical placement of the background.
CSS BACKGROUND-CLIP -ORIGIN. Defines which box the background paints into and is measured from. => border, content, padding
CSS BORDER-LEFT. Width of the left border edge; 0 disables it.
CSS BORDER-RIGHT. Width of the right border edge.
CSS BORDER-TOP. Width of the top border edge.
CSS BORDER-BOTTOM. Width of the bottom border edge — the four edges are independent.
CSS OUTLINE-WIDTH. Thickness of the outline drawn around the element; unlike borders it never affects layout.
CSS OUTLINE-OFFSET. Gap between element edge and outline — negative values pull it inside.
CSS OUTLINE-STYLE. Line style of the outline. => inset, outset, dotted, dashed
CSS BORDER-STYLE. Line style shared by all four border edges. => inset, outset, dotted, dashed
CSS BORDER-TOP-LEFT-RADIUS. Horizontal radius of the top-left corner.
CSS BORDER-TOP-LEFT-RADIUS. Vertical radius of the top-left corner — unequal values yield elliptical corners.
CSS BORDER-TOP-RIGHT-RADIUS. Horizontal radius of the top-right corner.
CSS BORDER-TOP-RIGHT-RADIUS. Vertical radius of the top-right corner.
CSS BORDER-BOTTOM-LEFT-RADIUS. Horizontal radius of the bottom-left corner.
CSS BORDER-BOTTOM-LEFT-RADIUS. Vertical radius of the bottom-left corner.
CSS BORDER-BOTTOM-RIGHT-RADIUS. Horizontal radius of the bottom-right corner.
CSS BORDER-BOTTOM-RIGHT-RADIUS. Vertical radius of the bottom-right corner.
CSS LAYOUT TRANSLATE. Horizontal shift of the element after layout — moves the rendering, not the layout slot.
CSS LAYOUT TRANSLATE. Vertical shift of the element after layout.
CSS LAYOUT SCALE. Horizontal scaling factor; 1 is natural size.
CSS LAYOUT SCALE. Vertical scaling factor — unequal x/y values distort deliberately.
CSS LAYOUT ROTATE. Rotation of the element around its transform origin.
CSS LAYOUT TRANSFORM-ORIGIN. Anchor point that rotate and scale pivot around. => top, left, right, bottom, angle nw, angle sw, angle ne, angle se
CSS LAYOUT JUSTIFY-CONTENT. Distribution of children along the main axis of this container. => start, ends, evenly, around, between
CSS LAYOUT ALIGN-CONTENT -ITEMS. Distribution and alignment of children along the cross axis. => ends, center, stretch, evenly, around, between
CSS LAYOUT FLEX-DIRECTION. Main axis of the container — row or column, optionally reversed.
CSS LAYOUT FLEX-WRAP. Whether children overflow onto new lines or stay on one.
CSS STYLE Z-INDEX. Stacking order relative to overlapping elements — higher values render on top.
CSS STYLE OVERFLOW. How content exceeding the element's bounds is handled, per axis or both. => both, axis x, axis y
CSS STYLE VISIBILITY DISPLAY. Toggles the element — hidden keeps its layout slot, none removes it entirely. => none, hidden, visible
CSS BACKGROUND-COLOR. Fill color of the element behind any background image.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Color of the first shadow layer defined above.
CSS STYLE BOX-SHADOW. Color of the second shadow layer.
CSS BORDER-COLOR. Color shared by all four border edges.
CSS OUTLINE-COLOR. Color of the outline.
CSS BACKGROUND-IMAGE. Selects the image painted inside the element.
CSS BACKGROUND-IMAGE. Repeat behavior of the background image.
CSS STYLE LINEAR-GRADIENT. Direction the linear gradient runs in. => top, left, right, bottom, angle nw, angle sw, angle ne, angle se
CSS STYLE RADIAL-GRADIENT. Shape of the radial gradient. => circle, ellipse, conical
CSS STYLE BORDER-IMAGE. Gradient type painted into the border. => linear, radial
CSS STYLE BORDER-IMAGE. Tuning value for the border gradient.
CSS STYLE LINEAR-GRADIENT -RADIAL. Tuning value shared by the gradient definitions above.
CSS STYLE BACKDROP-FILTER. Blur applied to whatever lies behind the element — frosted-glass effect.
CSS STYLE DROP-SHADOW. Horizontal offset of the filter-based shadow — unlike box-shadow it follows the rendered silhouette, including transparency.
CSS STYLE DROP-SHADOW. Vertical offset of the filter-based shadow.
CSS STYLE DROP-SHADOW. Blur radius of the filter-based shadow.
CSS STYLE BRIGHTNESS. Lightens or darkens the element as a whole.
CSS STYLE OPACITY. Overall transparency of the element and everything in it.
CSS STYLE SEPIA. Warms the element toward sepia tones.
CSS STYLE GRAYSCALE. Drains color toward monochrome.
CSS STYLE SATURATE. Intensifies or mutes the element's colors.
CSS STYLE CONTRAST. Widens or flattens the tonal range.
CSS STYLE HUE-ROTATE. Shifts every color around the hue wheel.

CSS MIX-BLEND-MODE. How the element's pixels combine with what lies beneath — 0 leaves normal compositing. => 1=hue, 2=color, 3=screen, 4=lighten, 5=darken, 6=overlay, 7=multiply, 8=saturation, 9=luminosity, 10=color dodge, 11=color burn, 12=hard light, 13=soft light
CSS STYLE ISOLATION. Creates a fresh stacking context — fences blend modes so they stop at this element instead of bleeding into the canvas below.
3-4: VARIOUS PROPERTIES. Image settings of the element — the image lives as a CSS background, so framing, cropping, and scaling are all driven from here.
CSS PADDING-LEFT. Left inset of the image frame; with the matching background-origin, padding crops into the image from this side.
CSS PADDING-RIGHT. Right inset of the image frame.
CSS PADDING-TOP. Top inset of the image frame.
CSS PADDING-BOTTOM. Bottom inset — the four sides are independent, allowing asymmetric framing.
CSS BACKGROUND-ORIGIN. Decides which box the image is measured against — and thus whether the paddings above frame it or crop it.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE. Base fitting mode of the image within the element. => contain, auto, 100%
CSS BACKGROUND-POSITION. Anchor preset for placing the image — coarse positioning before fine-tuning. => top, left, right, bottom, angle nw, angle sw, angle ne, angle se
CSS BACKGROUND-POSITION. Numeric horizontal offset — fine-tunes the placement relative to the anchor above.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE. Numeric horizontal scaling — overrides the fitting mode above for precise control.
CSS BACKGROUND-SIZE. Numeric vertical scaling; unequal values stretch the image deliberately.
CSS BACKGROUND-IMAGE. Selects which image this element renders — the file chosen via the PNG filetree.
3-5: VARIOUS PROPERTIES. Typography settings of the element's text field — every element from A00 to F99 carries one.
CSS PADDING-LEFT. Left inset of the text within its field.
CSS PADDING-RIGHT. Right inset of the text.
CSS PADDING-TOP. Top inset of the text.
CSS PADDING-BOTTOM. Bottom inset — the four sides are independent, so text can sit off-center deliberately.
CSS BACKGROUND-COLOR. Fill behind the text field — set for label-style plates, leave transparent to let the element's own styling show through.
CSS STYLE COLOR. Color of the text itself.
CSS ALIGN-SELF. Placement of the text field within the element along the cross axis. => start, end, center, stretch
CSS TEXT-ALIGN. Horizontal alignment of the text inside its field. => left, center, right, justify
CSS TEXT-DECORATION. Decorative lines applied to the text. => both, over, under
CSS FONT-WEIGHT. Weight of the type, from hairline to black — which cuts are available depends on the loaded font.
CSS FONT-SIZE LINE-HEIGHT. Type size and line spacing as a pair — the two values that set the text's vertical rhythm.
CSS FONT-FACE. Selects the typeface — one of the fonts loaded via the OTF filepicker.
HTML SPAN ENTITY. The text content itself — whatever this cell holds is what the element renders.

4-0.dir-tree-0: JSON FILE PICKER. Choose your base project file.
4-0.dir-tree-1: PNG FILE PICKER. Choose your base image file for canvas
4-0.dir-tree-2: FONT OTF FILE PICKER. Chose your base font file for canvas
4-0.button-0: CLEAR ALL DATA IN PROJECT JSON. Create a fresh clean state and start from scratch.
4-0.button-1: SET THE SEED FOR ALL 100 A-ELEMENTS. Reproduce the result on canvas faithfully on subsequent Regenerations.
4-0.button-2: SET THE SEED FOR ALL 100 B-ELEMENTS. Reproduce the result on canvas faithfully on subsequent Regenerations.
4-0.button-3: SET THE SEED FOR ALL 100 C-ELEMENTS. Reproduce the result on canvas faithfully on subsequent Regenerations.
4-0.button-4: GENERATE ART(WORK) ON CANVAS. Attention! will overwrite existing data, so export first if you want to keep your work.
4-0.button-5: SAVE IMAGE TO DISK. Both png and json file will be saved to where the app has been executed from.

4-1.dir-tree-0: FILE {f3} has been chosen and applied!
4-1.dir-tree-1: FILE {f3} has been chosen and applied!
4-1.dir-tree-2: FX {f3} has been chosen and applied!
4-1.button-0: ALL PARAMS have been erased, now start from scratch!
4-1.button-1: FX {f3}ACTIVATED Randomize Seed for A00 elements!
4-1.button-2: FX {f3}ACTIVATED Randomize Seed for B00 elements!
4-1.button-3: FX {f3}ACTIVATED Randomize Seed for C00 elements!
4-1.button-4: ARTWORK has been regenerated via random function!
4-1.button-5: PNG AND JSON FILES have been saved to disk!

4-2.delete: DELETE cell contents or files in local file picker.
